name: Saga Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the deep green of the Saga countryside, evoking growth and the prefecture's agricultural and forested landscape
the prefectural flower, the kusunoki (camphor) blossom, and purity

description: A deep green field bearing in the centre a white five-petalled camphor (kusunoki) blossom enclosing a red stylised mark representing 'Saga'.
history: Saga's prefectural symbol and flag were adopted on 22 May 1968; the symbol stylises the kusunoki (camphor) flower, the prefectural flower, and incorporates the shape of the name 'Saga'.

features.symbolism: A deep green field charged at the centre with a stylised camphor flower in white and red
a stylised five-petalled kusunoki (camphor) flower in white surrounding a red central element formed from the katakana ' ' / kanji shapes for 'Saga'; the camphor tree is the prefectural tree of Saga
